headline: Solar Feed-in Tariff Cuts Leave 12,000 Applications Stranded
context: SEDA Malaysia reduced the solar feed-in tariff by 38% in mid-2025, leaving approximately 12,000 approved but uninstalled residential applications economically unviable.

cards.big: SEDA approved 12,000 residential solar applications at RM0.57 per kWh.
cards.sub: Then cut the rate to RM0.35 before installation — making 78% of approved projects financially unviable.

cards.big: Malaysia's renewable energy target is 31% of capacity by 2025.
cards.sub: Actual achievement was 24.3%. Residential solar contributes just 3.1% of the total.

cards.big: Average payback period jumped from 8 years to 14 years overnight.
cards.sub: No transition provision or grandfathering clause was included in the revised tariff.

cards.big: TNB's reserve margin is 38% — among the highest in Southeast Asia.
cards.sub: Oversupply reduces the utility's incentive to accommodate distributed generation.

cards.big: You cannot build trust in renewable investment by changing the terms after approval.
Energy transition requires predictable policy. Retroactive tariff cuts do not save money — they destroy the investor confidence that drives adoption.
